title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in import-safe deterministic build pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.
Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.
Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Atomic Output
steps.instruction:
Order the Atomic Output Static-site publishing and Python source-to-publication evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Atomic Output.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Atomic Output assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Static-site publishing and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Atomic Output, keep Atomic Output connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug publishing team handles case 8854 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Atomic Output
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-atomic-output-5bjo8a" that uses case 7574 to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Atomic Output into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Atomic Output, keep Atomic Output connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-atomic-output-5bjo8a
# Competency: ssg-atomic-output.
# Case 7574: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_atomic_output_5bjo8a(stages: int = 10, completed: int = 10, import_safe: bool = True, staged_output: bool = True, prior_artifact_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_5bjo8a = "5bjo8a-a8ojb5-7574"
    assert evidence_variant_5bjo8a
    evidence_axes_5bjo8a = (
        "reader5bjo8a",
        "source7574",
        "parsea8ojb5",
        "route5bjo8a7574",
        "artifact75745bjo8a",
        "recoverya8ojb57574",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_5bjo8a)) == 6
    if stages < 8 or completed < 0:
        return False, completed, "pipeline-shape"
    if completed != stages:
        return False, completed, "partial-pipeline"
    if not import_safe or not staged_output:
        return False, completed, "effect-boundary"
    if not prior_artifact_kept:
        return False, completed, "destructive-output"
    assert completed == stages
    return True, completed, "atomic-pipeline"
